cases.prompt: open camoufox and go to example.com
just start the stealth browser
log into this site with a human-like browser
resume my saved camoufox profile and keep my logins
use camoufox to fill out the form on the dashboard
start a persistent anti-detect browser i can drive
open my stealth session and continue where i left off
am i detected as a bot
check my stealth browser for automation leaks
fire up camoufox headful and navigate to the reports page
do this task in a browser without getting flagged as automation
browse this site stealthily and read the page
install camoufox on this machine
camoufox fetch failed, fix the setup
run camoufox as a websocket server so other scripts can attach
keep the browser running and let my node client attach
expose camoufox over ws for cross-language clients
open a normal chrome browser with playwright
write a playwright test for the login page
set up xvfb on this linux server
what is the best anti-detect browser to use
take a screenshot of example.com
open camoufox and log into example.com
expected_behavior: runs the preflight and, if Camoufox is missing/unfetched, invokes camoufox-setup first
launches with persistent_context=True and a stable user_data_dir, headless=False, humanize=True
drives the login, then runs the built-in canary verification before trusting the session

cases.prompt: resume my saved stealth session
expected_behavior: relaunches with the SAME user_data_dir rather than creating a new profile
confirms persisted cookies/logins are available after restart
does not wipe or duplicate the profile

cases.prompt: am I detected?
expected_behavior: runs the bundled canary.py (ephemeral, or --ws against a running server)
reports the pass/fail scorecard and names any failed required check
maps a failure to its fix (headful vs headless, geoip/locale coherence, real wheel scroll)

cases.prompt: browse behind my US residential proxy with camoufox
expected_behavior: passes the proxy as a dict and enables geoip (or sets locale/timezone) so timezone and locale match the proxy geography
explains that a US IP with a non-US timezone is a detection seam and avoids it
warns if the geoip extra is missing rather than silently leaking a mismatch
